what_it_does: This file stores YAML-frontmatter metadata for a channel attachment, declaring it as a board_items attachment in the dragnet-development channel that points to board://item-8798b611 and recording its id and creation timestamp.
observations: The file contains only YAML frontmatter and no body content.
The target is a board:// URI referencing item-8798b611.
standouts: The target is expressed as a board://item-8798b611 URI.
The file is purely metadata with no payload or explanatory prose.
The attachment type board_items implies a link to a board artifact.
conventions: Uses YAML frontmatter in a Markdown file for structured metadata.
Follows a channel-memory attachment naming and storage convention.
Uses an ISO 8601 timestamp with explicit timezone offset.
Uses a custom board:// URI scheme to reference a board item.
concerns: The file carries no content or context beyond the linkage, so its meaning depends entirely on external board and channel data.
There is no author, source message, or lifecycle status recorded.
notes: A bare attachment record that links a dragnet-development channel attachment to a board item via a board:// URI.
questions: What is the semantic meaning of attachment_type board_items?
Who or what process created this attachment?
Is this attachment still current or has it been superseded?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose is to record a channel attachment, and the file does exactly that by capturing id, channel, type, target, and timestamp.
apparent_themes: Lightweight metadata records for cross-system traceability.
intention_signals: It appears designed to persist a typed, timestamped relationship between a channel memory attachment and a specific board item so the two can be correlated later.
novelty: The file is unusually minimal: it contains only linkage metadata and no narrative or payload, relying on a board:// URI scheme for target identity.
contribution: It contributes a precise, timestamped, typed edge between a channel attachment identifier and a board item, which a fused system can use to correlate channel memory with board artifacts.
summary_for_embedding: This is a YAML-frontmatter channel attachment metadata file that records a board_items attachment (att-654586be) in the dragnet-development channel pointing to board item item-8798b611, created at 2026-06-24T11:10:37.532645+00:00.
intention_for_embedding: It was created to persist a typed reference from a channel memory attachment to a specific board item for retrieval and traceability.
